name: managed-app-foundation-review
title: Managed App Foundation Review
version_notes: Initial first-party lightweight planning skill version with backend, automation, owner-hosted n8n, and upgrade-watch advisory guidance.

outputs.notes: Standalone skill README copied from first-party module source.
Standalone skill entrypoint copied from first-party module source.
OpenAI platform metadata copied from first-party module source.

skill_creation_review.existing_skill_review: Reviewed current skills and modules before adding managed-app-foundation-review: ui-ux-secure-frontend-design for frontend security and form review, self-hosted-service-safety for deployment exposure review, secure-cicd-installer for CI/CD setup, local-ai-stack-safety for local runtime exposure, codex-ssh-hostinger-coolify-setup-maintainer for Hostinger/Coolify server operations, n8n-agent-rules for n8n workflow safety, ai-coding-agent-rules routing, README skill tables, and the skill safety matrix. No existing skill owns the early product-architecture build-vs-buy decision for low-cost managed or owner-hosted auth, backend, database, automation, CRM, form, email, analytics, ops, and account foundations without becoming too broad.
skill_creation_review.trigger: Use managed-app-foundation-review when a user wants to revisit an implementation plan, cut custom build scope, compare build-vs-buy, choose low-cost/free managed or owner-hosted foundations, ask whether to browse current provider options, define usage-limit or upgrade-watch triggers, or decide whether to use existing CRM, auth, backend APIs, workflow automation, database, forms, email, storage, analytics, traffic/security monitoring, ops, or account-security services instead of building from scratch. Do not use for ordinary implementation after the foundation decision is already made.
skill_creation_review.decision_reason: The trigger is an early planning and security-risk-reduction decision, not frontend UI review, deployment safety, CI/CD setup, or localhost operations. A dedicated small skill prevents recurring custom auth/database/CRM rebuilds without bloating always-on coding rules.
skill_creation_review.unique_value: Forces a build/buy/skip/hybrid review before high-risk product, backend, data, automation, ops, and account primitives are custom-built, with explicit low-cost constraints, current-pricing verification from official docs when concrete recommendations are needed, owner-hosted n8n routing when suitable, upgrade-watch signals for later Codex review, and remaining security responsibilities.
skill_creation_review.runtime_footprint: SKILL.md is concise and instruction-only with no required long references, scripts, copied provider docs, or pricing tables; provider-specific details are checked live from official docs only when needed.
skill_creation_review.local_assets: No local assets beyond README, SKILL.md, and small platform metadata because provider pricing and limits change and must not be frozen into the toolkit.
skill_creation_review.output_contract: Return a compact Managed App Foundation Review with a build/buy/skip/hybrid table, low-cost or owner-hosted foundation candidates, security work avoided, remaining configuration, cost caveats, upgrade-watch thresholds, cut list, keep/build list, verify-before-commit list, security-still-required list, n8n safety note when self-hosted n8n is a candidate, and a note when current official pricing/docs were or still need to be checked.
skill_creation_review.anti_bloat_review: The skill is rejected for generic product brainstorming or ordinary implementation; it triggers only when foundation services, backend/automation/ops primitives, low-cost provider selection, scope cutting, or build-vs-buy security tradeoffs are in scope.
skill_creation_review.safety_boundary: Review-only by default. External account creation, provider configuration, DNS changes, production data migration, credentials, billing setup, paid-plan upgrades, deployment, or live-service mutation require explicit current-turn approval.
skill_creation_review.third_party_audit: not_applicable_first_party; no third-party text, provider docs, pricing tables, or code copied or adapted.
skill_creation_review.publisher_workflow: context-preserving-ai-publisher source-to-surface workflow with first-party _main source, deterministic copy outputs, routing updates, safety matrix updates, sync, and validation.
skill_creation_review.routing: managed-app-foundation-review is routed for early implementation-plan reviews involving managed or owner-hosted foundation selection, low-cost/free provider comparison, build-vs-buy scope cuts, usage-limit and upgrade-watch planning, auth/accounts/backend APIs/databases/workflow automation/CRM/forms/email/storage/analytics/ops foundations, and security work reduction. If self-hosted n8n workflow work begins, n8n-agent-rules owns the workflow safety boundary.

project.name: Managed App Foundation Review
project.summary: Small planning skill for deciding whether to use low-cost, free, managed, or owner-hosted foundations for auth, backend APIs, user accounts, databases, workflows/automation, CRM, forms, email, storage, analytics, usage-limit alerts, ops, and admin workflows instead of building those surfaces from scratch.

skill.summary: Review implementation plans for low-cost managed or owner-hosted foundations and upgrade triggers before custom-building auth, backend, database, workflow automation, CRM, form, email, analytics, ops, and account-security surfaces.
